required_skills: Curriculum design and end-to-end course development from needs analysis through deployment and evaluation
Instructional design theory and application — ADDIE, SAM, Backward Design, and competency-based frameworks
Learning outcome writing using Bloom's Taxonomy aligned to cognitive, affective, and psychomotor domains
E-learning content authoring using tools such as Articulate Storyline, Articulate Rise, or Adobe Captivate
Assessment design — formative and summative evaluation aligned to learning objectives and competency standards
Learner needs analysis and audience profiling to customize content for target learner demographics
Storyboarding and scripting for multimedia, video-based, and interactive digital learning modules
Collaboration with Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) to extract, structure, and validate domain knowledge for instructional purposes
preferred_skills: Experience designing curricula for professional certification programs, corporate L&D, or accredited academic programs
Familiarity with adaptive learning systems and AI-powered personalization platforms in EdTech
Understanding of learning analytics and using data to iterate on curriculum effectiveness and learner performance
Proficiency in video production tools and multimedia content creation for asynchronous learning delivery
Knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1, Section 508) and inclusive design principles for educational content
Experience with xAPI (Tin Can API), SCORM, or LTI standards for LMS-compliant content packaging
Exposure to microlearning design, spaced repetition frameworks, and gamification mechanics in digital learning
responsibilities: Lead end-to-end curriculum design projects — conducting needs analysis, defining learning architecture, sequencing content, developing materials, and managing quality assurance through final deployment
Write precise, measurable learning outcomes using Bloom's Taxonomy and map them rigorously to assessments, instructional activities, and competency standards across each module
Develop detailed instructional storyboards and content scripts for e-learning modules, video lectures, and interactive digital learning experiences in collaboration with media production teams
Work closely with Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) to extract domain knowledge, structure it for instructional purposes, and validate content accuracy through structured review cycles
Design aligned formative and summative assessments — including MCQs, scenario simulations, project briefs, and rubric-based evaluations — that measure learning outcomes effectively
Author and publish SCORM-compliant or xAPI-tracked e-learning modules using Articulate Storyline or Rise, ensuring content is LMS-ready and accessible across devices
Conduct learner needs analysis and audience research to customize instructional approach, language complexity, media mix, and scaffolding for the specific learner demographic
Implement curriculum quality assurance processes — peer reviews, pilot testing, learner feedback loops, and post-launch evaluations using the Kirkpatrick evaluation model
Use learning analytics and assessment performance data to identify curriculum gaps, low-engagement modules, and assessment misalignment, iterating on content to improve outcomes
Champion inclusive and accessible design practices across all curriculum outputs, ensuring compliance with WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards and universal design for learning (UDL) principles
